finding: Replay structure and coverage are independent evidence dimensions. A structurally valid replay must not authorize interpretation when thresholding or late observer installation leaves observation coverage partial.
design_implication: Use one fail-closed interpretation boundary that requires replay acceptance and absence-interpretable coverage; expose only privacy-bounded flags and reason codes.

verification.environment: isolated Node runtime using byte-identical committed module and test content plus fetched committed dependencies

observed: R-017 and R-018 were separate gates before this run
the new output excludes raw event and timing fields
inferred: partial coverage should block absence or responsiveness interpretation even when replay structure passes
proposed: Vercel Studio should invoke the composite gate immediately before any replay interpretation
superseded: consuming replay acceptance without a separate coverage decision

evidence_strength: deterministic contract test plus current W3C draft; no physical-browser evidence
falsification_route: show that the Event Timing specification exposes complete sub-threshold historical entries through buffered retrieval
show that a late-installed observer can prove no earlier interactions occurred
produce a conforming physical-browser packet where complete coverage is rejected by this classifier
demonstrate that privacy-bounded composite output cannot support the intended downstream decision
